feed.title: Engineering｜企業テックブログRSS
feed.subtitle: Engineeringセクションのブログ更新をまとめたRSSフィード

entry.title: Online index migration and shard-scaling in OpenSearch with AOSC | Inside Atlassian
entry.summary: Learn how Atlassian built AOSC, an OpenSearch plugin to reshape indices and scale their shards online. AOSC is a zero-downtime* index migration &amp; scaling engine for OpenSearch, built as an OpenSearch
entry.content: Learn how Atlassian built AOSC, an OpenSearch plugin to reshape indices and scale their shards online. AOSC is a zero-downtime* index migration &amp; scaling engine for OpenSearch, built as an OpenSearch plugin. It’s open source (Atlassian Labs repo, docs), and this post is the story of how, and why, we built it. Anyone who has […]The post Online index migration and shard-scaling in OpenSearch with AOSC appeared first on Inside Atlassian.

entry.title: How AI Rebuilt Salesforce’s Decades-Old Localization Pipeline | Salesforce Engineering Blog
entry.summary: In our Engineering Energizers Q&amp;A series, we highlight the engineering minds driving innovation across Salesforce. Today, we spotlight Teresa Marshall, Vice President of Localization. Teresa’s team de
entry.content: In our Engineering Energizers Q&amp;A series, we highlight the engineering minds driving innovation across Salesforce. Today, we spotlight Teresa Marshall, Vice President of Localization. Teresa’s team delivers every Salesforce release in up to 34 languages. As Salesforce engineering accelerated, product volume grew by more than 35% between releases while localization budgets and release windows remained […]The post How AI Rebuilt Salesforce’s Decades-Old Localization Pipeline appeared first on Sale

entry.title: Introducing Cache Response Rules | The Cloudflare Blog
entry.summary: Perhaps you’ve seen something that should sail out of cache get dragged back to the origin by a stray Set-Cookie or Cache-Control, headers that can be difficult to change on the origin itself. Cache R
entry.content: Perhaps you’ve seen something that should sail out of cache get dragged back to the origin by a stray Set-Cookie or Cache-Control, headers that can be difficult to change on the origin itself. Cache Response Rules is the fix, applied at the right time.

entry.title: Inside AI Builders Week: How teams work in the AI era | Inside Atlassian
entry.summary: Each quarter, we ask some of our R&amp;D teams to take a week away from the day-to-day to experiment and innovate on AI. We call this AI Builders Week, and it helps fuel our AI transformation. In past qua
entry.content: Each quarter, we ask some of our R&amp;D teams to take a week away from the day-to-day to experiment and innovate on AI. We call this AI Builders Week, and it helps fuel our AI transformation. In past quarters, we’ve focused on building for individual workflows — how do we each get faster and more productive […]The post Inside AI Builders Week: How teams work in the AI era appeared first on Inside Atlassian.

entry.title: Bitbucket Tests now supports tracking tests from multiple branches | Inside Atlassian
entry.summary: Tracking tests on non-default branches was one of the most requested capabilities during the Bitbucket Tests (beta). We heard you, and today, we’re shipping it. Most teams don’t ship from main. Integr
entry.content: Tracking tests on non-default branches was one of the most requested capabilities during the Bitbucket Tests (beta). We heard you, and today, we’re shipping it. Most teams don’t ship from main. Integration happens on develop, stabilisation on staging, releases cut from release. Until now, Bitbucket Tests only tracked the tests from default(main) branch, leaving the […]The post Bitbucket Tests now supports tracking tests from multiple branches appeared first on Inside Atlassian.

entry.title: Stop stitching together HR tools. Start Connecting Them. | Inside Atlassian
entry.summary: HR doesn’t have a tool problem, it has a connection problem. The Atlassian Ecosystem turns fragmented systems into one intelligent system of work. HR has quietly become one of the most cross-functiona
entry.content: HR doesn’t have a tool problem, it has a connection problem. The Atlassian Ecosystem turns fragmented systems into one intelligent system of work. HR has quietly become one of the most cross-functional operations in any organization. Onboarding a new hire touches IT provisioning, payroll, compliance training, manager coordination, and knowledge management, often across a half-dozen […]The post Stop stitching together HR tools. Start Connecting Them. appeared first on Inside Atlassian.

entry.title: Give AI agents the full picture with Loom video prompts | Inside Atlassian
entry.summary: Record your screen, talk through what you want done, and let Loom generate a structured action plan agents can use to execute tasks.The post Give AI agents the full picture with Loom video prompts app
entry.content: Record your screen, talk through what you want done, and let Loom generate a structured action plan agents can use to execute tasks.The post Give AI agents the full picture with Loom video prompts appeared first on Inside Atlassian.

entry.title: Securing Infrastructure at Scale: Introducing Pinterest’s Resource Provisioner Pipeline (RPP) | Pinterest Engineering Blog - Medium
How AI Reduced Customer Bug Triage from Nearly a Year to Less Than a Week | Salesforce Engineering Blog
entry.summary: By Priya Sethuraman, Abhishek Ghose, Lovish Agarwal, and Aditya Pandey. In our Engineering Energizers Q&amp;A series, we highlight the engineering minds driving innovation across Salesforce. Today, we spo
entry.content: By Priya Sethuraman, Abhishek Ghose, Lovish Agarwal, and Aditya Pandey. In our Engineering Energizers Q&amp;A series, we highlight the engineering minds driving innovation across Salesforce. Today, we spotlight Priya Sethuraman, Director of Software Engineering. Priya leads the Sales Cloud BugWiser initiative, an AI-powered system that standardizes customer bug classification and transforms customer bug signals into […]The post How AI Reduced Customer Bug Triage from Nearly a Year to Less Than a Wee

entry.title: How the 2026 World Cup affected Internet traffic | The Cloudflare Blog
entry.summary: We analyzed global HTTP traffic to explore how kickoff times, streaming habits, and hydration breaks reshaped online activity worldwide. From late-night traffic surges to halftime browsing spikes, her
entry.content: We analyzed global HTTP traffic to explore how kickoff times, streaming habits, and hydration breaks reshaped online activity worldwide. From late-night traffic surges to halftime browsing spikes, here is how the world connected during the global tournament.

entry.title: Analyzing the evidence that helps businesses win “product not received” disputes | Stripe Blog
entry.summary: To understand what can influence win rates, we analyzed evidence packets from one million disputes over a 16-week period. Here’s what the data shows and what it means for how you mitigate disputes.
entry.content: To understand what can influence win rates, we analyzed evidence packets from one million disputes over a 16-week period. Here’s what the data shows and what it means for how you mitigate disputes.

entry.title: Cloudflare Internal DNS is now generally available | The Cloudflare Blog
entry.summary: Cloudflare Internal DNS brings authoritative and recursive DNS for private networks to the same global network and control plane that runs Cloudflare's Zero Trust, networking, and public DNS.
entry.content: Cloudflare Internal DNS brings authoritative and recursive DNS for private networks to the same global network and control plane that runs Cloudflare's Zero Trust, networking, and public DNS.

entry.title: Materialized metrics in Prometheus | RedditEng
entry.summary: Written by Aleksandr Krivoshchekov and Walther Lee. Prometheus has a simple idea at its core: metric_name + labels = time_series. Any two samples with the same metric name and labels map to the same s
entry.content: Written by Aleksandr Krivoshchekov and Walther Lee. Prometheus has a simple idea at its core: metric_name + labels = time_series. Any two samples with the same metric name and labels map to the same series, but adding even a single extra label initiates a completely new series. In Kubernetes, that extra label is usually pod. It’s noisy and always changing. It spams our system with more and more series, slowing down queries and draining memory. It’s mostly pointless for our users. But it’s also c

entry.title: Content Ingestion &amp; Podcast Video Incident Report | Spotify Engineering
entry.summary: Over the past two months, podcast creators have experienced a series of reliability issues on Spotify. This...The post Content Ingestion &amp; Podcast Video Incident Report appeared first on Spotify Engin
entry.content: Over the past two months, podcast creators have experienced a series of reliability issues on Spotify. This...The post Content Ingestion &amp; Podcast Video Incident Report appeared first on Spotify Engineering.

entry.title: How our universal content processing platform Riviera evolved for AI and beyond | Dropbox Tech Blog
entry.summary: Riviera is the Dropbox content processing platform that’s been iteratively improving content transformation in our products for roughly a decade.
entry.content: Riviera is the Dropbox content processing platform that’s been iteratively improving content transformation in our products for roughly a decade.

entry.title: In-House LLM Serving at Netflix | Netflix TechBlog - Medium
Cloudflare WAF protects WordPress applications from two high-severity vulnerabilities | The Cloudflare Blog
entry.summary: Cloudflare has deployed two WAF rules in response to high-severity vulnerabilities disclosed to us by the WordPress security team. The new rules protect all Cloudflare customers using affected WordPre
entry.content: Cloudflare has deployed two WAF rules in response to high-severity vulnerabilities disclosed to us by the WordPress security team. The new rules protect all Cloudflare customers using affected WordPress versions, but customers should still update immediately to a patched release

entry.title: Closing the Loop: How to Build Self-Improving AI Systems with Automated Feedback Loops | Salesforce Engineering Blog
entry.summary: After six cycles, the system ran out of things to fix. That sentence probably raises more questions than it answers. Here is how we got there. The first skill PR took three days to merge. Twelve comme
entry.content: After six cycles, the system ran out of things to fix. That sentence probably raises more questions than it answers. Here is how we got there. The first skill PR took three days to merge. Twelve comments on structure. Six on conventions. The contributor fixed everything, merged, moved on. The next contributor hit the same […]The post Closing the Loop: How to Build Self-Improving AI Systems with Automated Feedback Loops appeared first on Salesforce Engineering Blog.

entry.title: The cost of saying yes has changed | The latest from GitHub's engineering team - The GitHub Blog
entry.summary: The cost of writing code dropped; the cost of owning it didn't. A framework for deciding which changes are actually cheap in the AI era.The post The cost of saying yes has changed appeared first on Th
entry.content: The cost of writing code dropped; the cost of owning it didn't. A framework for deciding which changes are actually cheap in the AI era.The post The cost of saying yes has changed appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
